sanskrit:
महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च।
इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः॥
transliteration: mahā-bhūtāny ahaṅkāro buddhir avyaktam eva ca | indriyāṇi daśaikaṃ ca pañca cendriya-gocarāḥ ||
literal_meaning: The five great elements, the ego, the intellect, the unmanifest, the ten senses plus one (mind), and the five objects of the senses.
contextual_meaning: Krishna gives Arjuna a phenomenological map of the human field: every sensation, thought, and feeling that can arise in consciousness is listed as part of the observable ‘field’, not the observer.
psychological_state: Stable metacognitive stance: the client can now label every inner event as ‘content’ rather than ‘self’, reducing fusion with symptoms.
trigger_scenario: A client in therapy overwhelmed by intrusive thoughts is asked to list every sensation, emotion, and thought they notice; this verse is the Sanskrit checklist the therapist silently uses.
anchor_text: Notice every thought and feeling as passing scenery, not as your identity.
